title: The Kid Brother

evidence.note: Published 1927; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

diy_links.label: Stanford Copyright Renewal DB — search “The Kid Brother” (renewals ~1954, 1955, 1956)
Catalog of Copyright Entries (archive.org) — browse the Motion Pictures renewal-year volume by hand
US Copyright Office public records (1978+ renewals/assignments)

editorial: The Kid Brother is a 1927 American silent comedy starring Harold Lloyd as the meek youngest son of a sheriff, directed by Ted Wilde and released by Paramount. As a 1927 publication, the film print is in the US public domain by expiry of the 95-year copyright term. Music and restoration layers remain undetermined pending their own evidence.
